book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
Decree with Regard to Nicomedia. Session XIII.
title: Decree with Regard to Nicomedia. Session XIII.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The most glorious judges said [after the reading of the imperial letters was finished]: These divine letters say nothing whatever with regard to the episcopate, but both refer to honour belonging to metropolitan cities.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: But the sacred letters of Valentinian and Valens of divine memory, which then bestowed metropolitan rights upon the city of Nice, carefully provided that nothing should be taken away from other cities.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: And the canon of the holy fathers decreed that there should be one metropolis in each province.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: What therefore is the pleasure of the holy synod in this matter?

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The holy synod cried out: Let the canons be kept.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Atticus the most reverend bishop of old Nicopolis in Epirus said: The canon thus defines, that a metropolitan should have jurisdiction in each province, and he should constitute all the bishops who are in that province.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: And this is the meaning of the canon.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Now the bishop of Nicomedia, since from the beginning this was a metropolis, ought to ordain all the bishops who are in that province.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The holy synod said: This is what we all wish, this we all pray for, let this everywhere be observed, this is pleasing to all of us.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: John, Constantine, Patrick [Peter] and the rest of the most reverend bishops of the Pontic diocese [through John who was one of them] said: The canons recognize the one more ancient as the metropolitan.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: And it is manifest that the most religious bishop of Nicomedia has the right of the ordination, and since the laws (as your magnificence has seen) have honoured Nice with the name only of metropolis, and so made its bishop superior to the rest of the bishops of the province in honour only.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The holy synod said: They have taught in accordance with the canons, beautifully have they taught.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: We all say the same things.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: The most glorious judges said: The most reverend the bishop of Nicomedia shall have the authority of metropolitan over the churches of the province of Bithynia, and Nice shall have the honour only of Metropolitical rank, submitting itself according to the example of the other bishops of the province of Nicomedia.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: For such is the pleasure of the Holy Synod.
